title: Unit, Property, Replay, Headless SDL, Render, and Playtest Evidence · Behavior Case: Playtest Accessibility
summary: Tests assert implementation details, use real time and global random state, compare one screenshot, never inject failures, and call dummy video proof of usability. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the playtest accessibility increment in risk-layered automated and human game test suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.
Test draw records, layers, transforms, asset bounds, and selected golden outputs with controlled tolerances and review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Asteroids team handles case 6101 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest. A defensible solution applies Playtest Accessibility, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to risk-layered automated and human game test suite.

content.code:
# Worked focus: asteroids-playtest-accessibility — Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-testing-headless-fixtures-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-testing-headless-fixtures-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_testing_headless_fixtures_5(unit_cases: int = 24, property_cases: int = 40, headless_cases: int = 9, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, dummy_driver_verified: bool = True, render_contract_checked: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, property_cases, headless_cases) < 1:
        return False, unit_cases, "test-layers"
    if not changed_defect_detected:
        return False, unit_cases, "mutation-survived"
    if not dummy_driver_verified:
        return False, headless_cases, "headless-gap"
    if not render_contract_checked:
        return False, headless_cases, "render-gap"
    total = unit_cases + property_cases + headless_cases
    assert total >= 3
    return True, total, "risk-layered-game-tests"

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Sequence Playtest Accessibility evidence · Theory Playtest Accessi…, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Playtest Accessibility precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Sequence Playtest Accessibility evidence · Theory Playtest Accessi…, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 5196 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Sequence Playtest Accessibility evidence · Theory Playtest Accessi…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Automated accessibility checks can certify game playability.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Playtest Accessibility model · Theory Playtest Accessib…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Playtest Accessibility: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 6815 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer pure unit and property tests, deterministic replay, headless SDL integration, render contracts, changed defects, and manual accessibility playtests; preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Playtest Accessibility model · Theory Playtest Accessib….
steps.why: Retrieving Playtest Accessibility through the concrete risk-layered automated and human game test suite case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Playtest Accessibility model · Theory Playtest Accessib…, keep Playtest Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Run structured tasks with varied players and devices, capture barriers and observations ethically, repair, and retest.
